Class TimeManager

java.lang.Object
simcli.engine.TimeManager
public class TimeManager extends Object
Tracks the simulation clock — ticks, days, and time-of-day.

Time progresses in discrete ticks. A configurable number of ticks (ticksPerDay) constitutes one in-game day. The manager can compute the current day number, time-of-day period (Morning / Afternoon / Evening / Night), formatted hour string, and day-of-week name from the raw tick counter.

Ticks advance via advanceTick() (single) or advanceTicks(int) (bulk). Bulk advancement is used when the Sim sleeps — the clock jumps forward to the next morning.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    TimeManager(int ticksPerDay)
    Creates a TimeManager starting at tick 1.
    TimeManager(int currentTick, int ticksPerDay)
    Creates a TimeManager restoring a previously saved tick.

  • Constructor Details

    • TimeManager

      public TimeManager(int ticksPerDay)
      Creates a TimeManager starting at tick 1.
      Parameters:
      ticksPerDay - number of ticks that constitute one in-game day (e.g. 24 for one tick per simulated hour).

    • TimeManager

      public TimeManager(int currentTick, int ticksPerDay)
      Creates a TimeManager restoring a previously saved tick.
      Parameters:
      currentTick - the tick counter to restore.
      ticksPerDay - number of ticks that constitute one in-game day.

  • Method Details

    • advanceTick

      public void advanceTick()
      Advances the simulation clock by a single tick.

    • advanceTicks

      public void advanceTicks(int ticks)
      Advances the simulation clock by the given number of ticks.
      Parameters:
      ticks - the number of ticks to advance; must be ≥ 0.

    • getCurrentTick

      public int getCurrentTick()
      Returns the raw tick counter.

    • getCurrentDay

      public int getCurrentDay()
      Returns the current in-game day number (1-indexed).

    • getTimeOfDay

      public String getTimeOfDay()
      Returns the current time-of-day period based on the tick position within the current day.
      Returns:
      one of "Morning", "Afternoon", "Evening", or "Night".

    • getFormattedTime

      public String getFormattedTime()
      Returns a formatted hour string (e.g. "08:00") derived from the tick position within the current day.
      Returns:
      hour in HH:00 format.

    • getDayOfWeek

      public String getDayOfWeek()
      Returns the day-of-week name derived from the current day number. Cycles through Monday–Sunday.
      Returns:
      the day-of-week name.
